text:
Author's Note

Translation

Many of my possessions that have been through the days with me ever since the beginning of this serialization are all starting to break one after the other these days.
The large pot that I shared with my assistant, my stereo components, my anti-back pain chair, etc...
You guys!!
How could you just fall like this when we're still in the midst of battle!! (Cry)
Chapters Included

Chapter 79 "Bite Of The Ant" (蟻のひと噛み Ari no Hito Kami)
Chapter 80 "The Prodigal Father Returns" (瞼の父 Mabuta no Chichi)
Chapter 81 "A Full Recovery" (バリバリの全開 Baribari no Zenkai)
Chapter 82 "Family By Spirit" (魂の家族 Tamashii no Kazoku)
Chapter 83 "The Promised Day" (約束の日 Yakusoku no Hi)

Synopsis

From the back of the volume:

"Betrayal and double-crossing are the order of the day. Greed, Pride and Envy prove to be formidable enemies, but one homunculus seems to be having pangs of--could it be?--conscience. Ominous news spreads quickly about a mysterious portal. Will this gateway help the Elric brothers regain their natural bodies? And will the gigantic underground transmutation circle be completed, leading to a major regime change? Plus, a moving reunion between Al and someone from his past!"

Spine

Outside Cover

Greed (In Ling Yao's body)

Inside Cover (In Memoriam)

Bido

Omake

Trivia

Though countless Drachma soldiers and the commander of the Drachma army himself died in this volume, they were not depicted on the spine of this volume in memoriam.
In this volume, Hiromu Arakawa announced that a second anime adaptation was being produced. This series would become Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood.
